output:
**(B).**

All three are instances of **throughput-quality inversion** — optimizing the visible flow rate degrades first-pass quality, which creates a hidden return loop (readmissions, repeat calls, regressions) that feeds demand back into the system's intake. The metric improves while the system's actual output degrades.

The key structural element is the **rework loop**: the "completed" items aren't actually completed, so they re-enter the queue, inflating the very load the optimization was meant to reduce.
